Resource Library

Author

Claudius Gräbner-Radkowitsch

The Methods Hub resource library is a searchable collection of individual pages covering research methods in the social sciences. Each page addresses one task or concept — working through a page takes roughly 20–40 minutes. Resources are written at BA level by default, with MA/PhD extensions where relevant.

Use the filters below to browse by topic, level, or content type.

Data Preparation with the Tidyverse
R
data-cleaning
tidyverse
BA
tutorial

How to reshape data between wide and long format with tidyr, manipulate variables and filter rows with dplyr, merge data sets, and chain steps together using pipes.

Jun 16, 2026

Experiments in R
R
experiments
statistics
BA
tutorial

How to analyze experimental data in R: t-tests for simple comparisons, one-way and two-way ANOVA for multi-group designs, effect sizes, post-hoc tests, and power analysis.

Jun 16, 2026

First Steps in R
R
BA
tutorial

Learn the basic syntax of R: how to issue commands, use objects, assignments, and functions, and how to install and use R packages.

Jun 16, 2026

Getting Started with Quarto
R
quarto
reproducibility
BA
tutorial

An introduction to Quarto — the modern successor to R Markdown — covering how to create documents that mix text and code, control output rendering, and produce HTML and PDF…

Jun 16, 2026

Importing and Exporting Data in R
R
data-cleaning
BA
tutorial

How to read CSV files into R using data.table::fread(), handle common format variations (separators, decimal signs, leading zeros), and save data to CSV, RDS, RData, and…

Jun 16, 2026

Getting Started with R: Installing Everything You Need
R
BA
tutorial

Step-by-step guide to installing R, RStudio, Git, Quarto, and registering for GitHub and Netlify so you have a complete working environment for data science.

Jun 16, 2026

Installing R Packages
R
BA
tutorial

How to install the R packages needed for data science work, verify your installation using the DataScienceExercises package, and set up LaTeX for PDF output.

Jun 16, 2026

Linear Regression in R
R
regression
statistics
BA
tutorial

Practical implementation of simple and multiple linear regression in R: fitting models with lm(), interpreting coefficients, computing R², understanding omitted variable…

Jun 16, 2026

Logarithms: A Gentle Introduction
R
statistics
regression
BA
tutorial

A practical introduction to logarithms for data science: what they are, the natural logarithm, the relationship with exponential functions, and when and why to use log…

Jun 16, 2026

Object Types in R I: Functions
R
BA
tutorial

An introduction to functions as R objects: how to call them in prefix and infix form, how to work with arguments, and how to define and document your own functions.

Jun 16, 2026

Object Types in R II: Vectors
R
BA
tutorial

A thorough introduction to atomic vectors and lists in R: types (logical, integer, double, character), coercion, logical operations, vectorization, indexing, and useful…

Jun 16, 2026

Object Types in R III: Factors and Data Frames
R
BA
tutorial

An introduction to factors and data frames in R: how they are built upon base types, how to create and manipulate them, and the tibble as a modern alternative.

Jun 16, 2026

Regression Residuals and Model Diagnostics
R
regression
statistics
BA
tutorial

Understand the distinction between residuals and error terms, learn the key assumptions of linear regression, and apply visual and quantitative diagnostic tests to evaluate…

Jun 16, 2026

Setting Up an R Project
R
BA
tutorial

How to create a well-structured R project directory, understand working directories and file paths, and use the here package to write portable, reproducible code.

Jun 16, 2026

Using AI Tools for R Coding
R
BA
reading

A practical overview of how to use AI assistants effectively for R programming tasks — from generating first-draft scripts to debugging — including how to write good prompts…

Jun 16, 2026

Visualization with ggplot2
R
visualization
BA
tutorial

How to build publication-quality plots in R using ggplot2: the layer-by-layer workflow, mapping aesthetics, adding geoms, adjusting scales, themes, and saving plots.

Jun 16, 2026

PLACEHOLDER: What is Epistemology? (BA)
epistemology
BA
concept

A first introduction to epistemology — the branch of philosophy concerned with how we know what we know — and why it matters for social science research.

Jun 15, 2026

PLACEHOLDER: Introduction to Systematic Literature Reviews
literature-review
BA
MA
reading

An overview of what a systematic literature review is, how it differs from a narrative review, and how to plan and execute one.

Jun 15, 2026

statistics
R
BA
MA
reading

An overview of probability distributions beyond the normal: binomial, Poisson, uniform, exponential, beta, and gamma — with guidance on when to use each and how to work with…

Jun 15, 2026

Conceptual Foundations of Statistics
statistics
BA
MA
concept

A first orientation to statistical thinking: what statistics is, why intuition alone is insufficient for interpreting data, and how probability theory provides the reference…

Jun 15, 2026

statistics
BA
MA
concept

An introduction to the building blocks of data: types of variables (categorical vs. numerical), scales of measurement (nominal, ordinal, interval, ratio), and the…

Jun 15, 2026

Descriptive Statistics in R
statistics
R
tidyverse
BA
MA
reading

A guided introduction to descriptive statistics in R, covering measures of centrality, dispersion, and association — including correlation matrices, simple linear…

Jun 15, 2026

Hypothesis Testing and Confidence Intervals
statistics
R
BA
MA
reading

Applying sampling theory to make inferences: how to evaluate claims using data (hypothesis testing and p-values) and how to construct ranges of plausible values for…

Jun 15, 2026

Essentials of Probability Theory for Statistics
statistics
R
BA
MA
reading

A conceptual and practical introduction to probability theory: random experiments, events, conditional probability, random variables, the normal distribution, expected…

Jun 15, 2026

Sampling and Statistical Inference
statistics
R
BA
MA
reading

How we move from sample statistics to conclusions about populations: sampling variability, Monte Carlo simulation, sampling distributions, standard error, the Central Limit…

    Source Code
    ---
title: "Resource Library"
listing:
  id: resource-listing
  type: grid
  contents: "*/*.qmd"
  filter-ui: true
  sort-ui: true
  categories: true
  sort: "date desc"
  fields: [image, title, description, categories, date]
  page-size: 24
---

The Methods Hub resource library is a searchable collection of individual pages covering research methods in the social sciences. Each page addresses one task or concept — working through a page takes roughly 20–40 minutes. Resources are written at BA level by default, with MA/PhD extensions where relevant.

Use the filters below to browse by topic, level, or content type.

```{=html}
<div class="d-flex justify-content-end mb-2">
  <div class="btn-group btn-group-sm" role="group" aria-label="View toggle">
    <button id="view-btn-grid" type="button" class="btn btn-outline-secondary active">
      <i class="fa-solid fa-grip"></i> Grid
    </button>
    <button id="view-btn-list" type="button" class="btn btn-outline-secondary">
      <i class="fa-solid fa-list"></i> List
    </button>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {#resource-listing}
:::

```{=html}
<script>
(function () {
  function setView(view) {
    var el = document.getElementById('listing-resource-listing');
    var btnGrid = document.getElementById('view-btn-grid');
    var btnList = document.getElementById('view-btn-list');
    if (!el || !btnGrid || !btnList) return;
    var isGrid = view === 'grid';
    el.classList.toggle('list-mode', !isGrid);
    btnGrid.classList.toggle('active', isGrid);
    btnList.classList.toggle('active', !isGrid);
    try { localStorage.setItem('resourceView', view); } catch (e) {}
  }

  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
    var saved;
    try { saved = localStorage.getItem('resourceView'); } catch (e) {}
    setView(saved || 'grid');
    document.getElementById('view-btn-grid').addEventListener('click', function () { setView('grid'); });
    document.getElementById('view-btn-list').addEventListener('click', function () { setView('list'); });
  });
}());
</script>
```

    © 2026 Claudius Gräbner-Radkowitsch
